results.title: EQK: Mac app with dynamic AI EQ
results.content:
Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm Aakash, the solo dev behind EQK (and KUJA.dev). This started with a small, dumb frustration. My headphones sounded great for one thing and wrong for another, and macOS gives you exactly one flat curve for the entire system. So I'd fix my music, then a video would sound harsh, then a song would sound thin. One machine, one sound, no nuance. That never made sense to me- every app, every track, every pair of headphones is different. So the problem I set out to solve was simple to say and annoying to build: let every app on your Mac have its own sound, and stop making people babysit a single EQ curve. The first version was static- per-app EQ plus AutoEq headphone correction. Useful, but I kept nudging bands manually every time a different genre came on, and I realized [...] Subscribe

Launching today

EQK

# EQK

Launching today

## Mac app with dynamic AI EQ

7 followers

## Mac app with dynamic AI EQ

7 followers

Visit website

EQK is the first Mac app with dynamic AI EQ - an equalizer that re-tunes itself per song in real time. Give every app its own EQ, headphone correction, and output device. No virtual drivers - just native macOS audio capture. 10-band parametric Tone Desk, 3,985+ AutoEq headphone profiles, per-app limiter, full signal-chain metering, and a free built-in FLAC player with synced lyrics for the songs that you own. 100% local, no telemetry, no subscription. Tune every app on your Mac.

 Overview
 Reviews

 Built with
 Team
 More

Free Options

Launch tags:Music•Artificial Intelligence•Entertainment

Launch Team / Built With [...] I care about a lot: no virtual audio drivers (it uses native macOS audio capture), and 100% local - audio never leaves your Mac, no telemetry, no accounts. It's built by one person, so I'd genuinely love your feedback - what breaks, what's missing, what headphones you want profiles for. I'm here all day. 🙏

results.title: Large-Scale Analysis of User Feedback on AI-Powered ...
results.content:
This analysis reveals that even the most celebrated features are frequently accompanied by specific complaints. For in-stance, users are impressed by the AI’s conversational depth and utility, while also noting occasional lapses in factual accuracy: “Outstanding. The AI is articulate, reasonable, help-ful, and intuitive. It’s also got a detectably layered social aspect, as with a real person. It’s not perfect – it sometimes gets well-established facts and simple math wrong, so you have to stay on your toes · · ·” Similarly, reviews demonstrate that users praise the app’s overall utility, yet express frustration with the scanner’s limitations and occasional errors: “This app very useful and very good but there is many problems in this when we scan math problem it can’t identify the text · [...] VI. IMPLICATIONS Drawing on the empirical findings of RQ1 – RQ3, we derive a set of actionable recommendations that address both overall and category-specific user pain points.
(a) Strengthen reliability in core AI features. Failures in scanning, OCR, and vision collectively represent some of the most common sources of user frustration across multiple categories. Scanner-related issues account for over 13.2K negative aspects and are among the leading complaints in Tools, Productivity, Education, Health & Fit-ness, and Lifestyle apps. To address this, developers should prioritize rigorous validation, continuous monitoring, and user-centered error handling for scanning features. [...] Dual-Role Topics Reveal User Expectations. A notable finding from the cluster analysis is that several features, such as Chat, Voice, Scanner, Math, Answer Quality and Language, receive both positive and negative feedback. This duality shows the pivotal role these functionalities play in shaping user experience and reflects the high expectations users have for AI features.
For instance, the Chat functionality frequently serves as both a differentiator for user satisfaction and a source of frustration.

• FAMILY & LIFE: Set boundaries with parents. Talk money with your partner. Handle teen screen time battles. Navigate caregiver conflicts. Deal with difficult neighbors. Wedding planning disagreements. Inheritance disputes.

• SOCIETY: Bureaucratic nightmares. Online trolls. Neighbor noise disputes. Standing up to bias and stigma. Customer service escalations. Age discrimination.
Each scenario features a distinct AI character with realistic personality — defensive, dismissive, guilt-tripping, passive-aggressive. They push back. They deflect. They gaslight. Just like real people.

results.title: Common Reasons AI Apps Are Rejected by Google Play & Apple (How to Fix) | Artificial Intelligence in Plain English
results.content:
## Play Store and App Store Rejections You’ll Likely Face

When you deal with generative AI apps, the reviewers will stress-test your product like crazy. They intentionally push the app to its limits with risky prompts, test payment policies, and even check if you’ve built proper moderation.

# Why AI Apps Keep Getting Rejected (and Nobody Talks About It)

Part Time Developer

Part Time Developer

4 min read

·

Sep 11, 2025

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Google Play Rejections

Press enter or click to view image in full size

AppStore Rejections [...] AppStore Rejections

Hello, I’m a part-time vibe coder 😂 . A few months ago, I launched an AI Content Generation App where users can create images, audio, and videos. Fast forward 4 months — my app is live on both the Play Store and App Store, has crossed 45k+ downloads, and is generating over $5,000 in MRR.

Sounds smooth, right? Well, not exactly. Behind these numbers were endless rejections, confusing policies, and some facepalm moments. What I noticed is that nobody really talks about the rejection side of AI apps — especially for indie creators and solo developers.

So, here’s the stuff I wish I knew earlier. If you’re building and releasing your own AI-powered app, this guide should save you a lot of headaches.

## Play Store and App Store Rejections You’ll Likely Face

Wow! thank you Apple! I’m thrilled to see our team’s hard work featured on Apple’s M5 MacBook Pro page today.   
  
Our plugin MicDrop is a realtime AI vocal transformation AU/VST3 that allows you to transform your voice into (now) up to 80+ different singers and instruments. On Apple Silicon it’s now faster, more high fidelity and performant than ever.   
  
You can even play a new melody on a MIDI keyboard.   
  
Congratulations to our founding team - Lacy, Dr. Michael Hetrick and Joshua Dickinson. Thank you Matt Higgins for your tremendous belief, friendship and guidance and Jerin and Nhan for your amazing hard work!
